target.label: egress policy — dnsmasq + nftables (fail-closed, IPv4/IPv6 parity, atomic reload)

rationale: Infrastructure-security class story — third Epic 2 + first of its class (daemon + kernel-rule + file-locked atomic-reload + JSONL log-tailer outside the Vitest/Playwright idiom); extends the Story 2.2 iter-149 twelfth cumulative WAIVED precedent (Stories 1.7 iter-4 + 1.8 iter-3 + 1.9 iter-3 + 1.10 iter-46 + 1.11 iter-57 + 1.12 iter-64 + 1.13 iter-71 + 1.14 iter-78 + 1.15 iter-85 + 1.16 iter-92 → 2.1 iter-126 → 2.2 iter-149 → 2.3 iter-159 = THIRTEENTH cumulative trace-WAIVED; under the ATDD-skip-trace-WAIVED co-application rule this pairs with the FOURTEENTH iter-157 ATDD-skip: 10 Epic-1 pairings + 2.1 pairing + 2.2 pairing + 2.3 pairing = 14th pairing overall). Story v1.2 iter-157 Change Log row (ATDD-skip) explicitly defers per-AC automated coverage to Story 2.3-test-runner-landing (Epic 13 scope) + Task 12.1 – 12.8 operator-workstation live smokes (backend-B iteration-env constraint — kernel-nftables-privilege absent in DinD host-socket-passthrough layer + bind-mount denial precedent from Story 2.1 iter-127) via hybrid ground-(a)-(b)-(c)-(d) variant-(ii)+(iii) ATDD-skip clause. (a) substrate-verification-covers-ACs at CLI-exit-code level — (AC 1) packages/devbox/dnsmasq/dnsmasq.conf authored iter-158 (2592 bytes) with fail-closed `address=/#/0.0.0.0` + `address=/#/::` + listen-address 127.0.0.1,::1:53 + user=nobody privilege-drop + log-queries=extra + KEEL_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST_MARKER_{START,END} marker block per SC-12; start-egress.sh SC-13 /etc/resolv.conf pin to 'nameserver 127.0.0.1' + 'options edns0 single-request-reopen' + 5s dnsmasq-liveness retry with fail-hard exit 1; .envrc.example line 43-44 KEEL_DEVBOX_DNS_UPSTREAM=1.1.1.1 knob per SC-14; Dockerfile lines 56-57 dnsmasq apt-append; entrypoint.sh line 101 single new fail-hard block per SC-11; (AC 2) packages/devbox/nftables/egress.nft authored iter-158 (3605 bytes) with single `table inet keel_egress` + `chain output_v4 { type filter hook output priority 0; policy drop }` + `chain output_v6 { type filter hook output priority 0; policy drop }` + `meta nfproto != <family> accept` short-circuit first rule per-chain (iter-158 non-obvious decision: same-priority inet chains otherwise double-drop non-target-family packets) + baseline ct state established,related accept + loopback 127.0.0.1:53 allow + KEEL_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST_MARKER block; docker-compose.yml lines 91-99 cap_add [NET_ADMIN, NET_RAW] per SC-6 with stale TODO-comment replacement per Task 9.1; Dockerfile nftables apt-append; (AC 3) egress-log-tailer.sh authored iter-158 (7377 bytes; bash -n exit 0 at iter-159) with tail -Fn0 follow-rotation + 3-format dnsmasq parser (query/reply/config) + SC-3 6-field JSONL schema EMBEDDED VERBATIM in docs/invariants/devbox-egress.md § JSONL query log schema per iter-156 PATCH 3 (contentHash aad16a51aa1dc7527c0312e6b99217966d7f3f3478fb677dd347792e9cb6889b locks 6-field contract against drift) + SC-2 /workspace/logs/egress-queries.jsonl pinned path + SC-4 50 MB + 5 gzip generations inline rotation + SC-15 nohup background process model with SIGTERM/SIGINT flush-close + SC-16 reload/rotation orthogonality + parse-error fallback with raw-line preservation; monitor.sh operator `tail -Fn0 | jq -c` facade; (AC 4) reload-egress.sh authored iter-158 (8342 bytes; bash -n exit 0 at iter-159) with SC-5 atomic-reload verbatim — flock -x /run/keel-egress.lock 10s timeout + nft -f <tempfile> kernel-atomic transaction + kill -HUP $(cat /run/dnsmasq.pid) with pkill -HUP fallback + trap-released flock + 8 documented exit codes covering argument contract / lock timeout / nft-f / render / SIGHUP failure modes per Task 6.2 – 6.9; SC-8 whitelist-compose-boundary preserved (reload-egress.sh takes composed-whitelist-path arg; whitelist.sh user-facing CLI is Story 2.4 scope); (AC 5) dual-layer fail-closed verified at template level — (layer 1) dnsmasq `address=/#/` NXDOMAIN + (layer 2) nftables `output_v4/v6 policy drop` layer-3 rejection; SC-13 resolv.conf pin (NO 8.8.8.8 fallback — closes upstream cc-devbox bug #2); SC-1 in-container runtime (NOT sidecar — matches AC 1 verbatim + architecture § S5 l.224); SC-8 single-mechanism collapse (closes upstream bug #1 divergent-whitelist-tooling); IPv4/IPv6 parity verbatim (closes upstream bug #3 IPv6 default-deny gap); README § Known upstream bugs fixed 3-line documentation. All 4 new scripts + entrypoint.sh bash -n syntax OK at iter-159. Sync-gate (node packages/keel-invariants/dist/check.js) exit 0 green at iter-159 with 20 manifest entries valid including INV-devbox-egress-contract new entry. (b) no test runner at Story 2.3 time — Epic 13 scope; recursive probe for vitest/jest/playwright/cypress configs returns zero matches at iter-159; stack detection returns none (package.json has no react/vue/angular/next/playwright/cypress/vitest/jest dependencies — Epic 13 delivers framework landing per prior twelve precedents). (c) HYBRID variant-(ii)+(iii) — variant (ii) downstream-test-runner-landing-covers-per-AC-coverage: Story 2.3-test-runner-landing (Epic 13 scope) + operator-workstation live smokes (Task 12.1 – 12.8) will unlock per-AC runtime probes (resolv.conf pin + pgrep dnsmasq + SC-7 verbatim nft list chain parity + dual-layer positive/negative curl + atomic-reload preservation + JSONL schema round-trip + log-rotation threshold); Story 2.4 whitelist CLI + Story 2.5 hardening + Story 2.6 host-side pnpm + Story 2.13 healthcheck + Epic 4 FR37 security-evidence consumer integration-test in their landing contexts; variant (iii) spec-declared-CR-substitution — Story 2.3 § Dev-agent guardrails (12 MUST-follow rules: fail-closed-everywhere + atomic-reload-kernel-level + IPv4/IPv6-parity-verbatim + JSONL-schema-stable + scope-isolation + entrypoint-minimal-surgery + invariant-drift-discipline + no-runtime-installs + kebab-case-sh-0755 + backend-B-aware + no-.envrc/.envrc.local-edits + single-source-of-truth) + 17 pinned scope-clarifications (SC-1 through SC-17) + forthcoming /bmad-code-review (args: "2") adversarial envelope (Blind Hunter / Edge Case Hunter / Acceptance Auditor fan-out against cumulative Story 2.3 substrate diff: ~650 LOC across 11 new files + 11 modified files) substitute for red-phase scaffolds. (d) upstream-provenance-precedent — Story 2.1 iter-98 established ground-(d) for infrastructure-smoke class stories absorbing upstream cc-devbox content: upstream cc-devbox has no test suite, absorbing preserves that posture faithfully; Story 2.3 inherits this ground-(d) via its extension of Story 2.1's fail-closed egress scaffold — Story 2.3 IS the fix for upstream cc-devbox's three documented bugs (divergent-whitelist-tooling + fail-open-resolv.conf + IPv6-default-deny-gap). The deterministic FAIL signal (overall 0% < 80%) is a structural false-positive; no test runner is wired at Story 2.3 substrate stage. THIRTEENTH CUMULATIVE TRACE-WAIVED PRECEDENT (Stories 1.7 → 1.16 = 10; Story 2.1 = 11; Story 2.2 = 12; Story 2.3 = 13) — first infrastructure-security class precedent-holder (Story 2.1 was infrastructure-smoke; Story 2.2 was hybrid infrastructure-smoke + configuration-surface; Story 2.3 is a new class with daemon + kernel-rule + atomic-reload + log-tailer outside the Vitest/Playwright idiom). Structural trace-artefact inheritance from Stories 2.1 + 2.2 (~95% reasoning-cost savings per iter-126 + iter-149 template; mechanical adaptation to 2.3's 5-AC infrastructure-security class with dnsmasq + nftables + JSONL-emitter-specific evidence replacing dotfile-schema + compose-parameterisation evidence). Unlike Story 2.2's fully-in-iteration-executable substrate (docker compose config is pre-daemon YAML-time), Story 2.3 inherits Story 2.1's operator-owned carve-out pattern for runtime container smokes: backend-B iteration-env kernel-nftables-privilege absence + bind-mount denial force every live smoke (Task 12.1 – 12.8) to operator workstation, following Story 2.1 iter-144 DEFER-annotation precedent. Story 2.3 advances Story State `in-dev → traced` (FR14n lifecycle matrix); next QUEUE item: `/bmad-create-story (args: "review")` post-dev SM requirements-satisfaction verification (`traced → sm-verified` or `sm-fixes-pending`).
